searchText: gate cse 2026 | set 1 | question: 40 algorithms 2026 set 1 gatecse-2026-set1 algorithms depth-first-search two-marks multiple-selects minimum-spanning-tree binary-heap one-mark hashing linear-probing goclasses gate2026_cs_set1_memorybased graph-algorithms consider the following pseudocode for depth-first search (dfs) algorithm which takes a directed graph $g(v, e)$ as input, where $d[v]$ and $f[v]$ are the discovery time and finishing time, respectively, of the vertex $v \in v$. \[ \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.3} \begin{array}{|p{7cm}|p{7cm}|} \hline \textbf{dfs}(g): \begin{array}{l} \text{unmark all } v \in v \\ t \leftarrow 0 \\ \text{for each } v \in v \\ \quad \text{if } v \text{ is unmarked} \\ \qquad t \leftarrow \text{explore}(g,v,t) \\ \quad \text{end if} \\ \text{end for} \end{array} & \textbf{explore}(g,v,t): \begin{array}{l} \text{mark } v \\ t \leftarrow t+1 \\ d[v] \leftarrow t \\ \text{for each } (v,w) \in e \\ \quad \text{if } w \text{ is unmarked} \\ \qquad t \leftarrow \text{explore}(g,w,t) \\ \quad \text{end if} \\ \text{end for} \\ t \leftarrow t+1 \\ f[v] \leftarrow t \\ \text{return } t \end{array} \\ \hline \end{array} \] suppose that the input directed graph $g(v, e)$ is a directed acyclic graph (dag). for an edge $(u, v) \in e$, which of the following options will never be correct?

\begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline {\textbf{sdd1}} \\ \hline { \begin{array}{c} \textbf{grammar} \\ (g_1) \end{array}} & {\textbf{semantic rules}} \\ \hline \hline d \rightarrow tv & \begin{array}{l} d.type = t.type \\ v.type = t.type \end{array} \\ \hline t \rightarrow \text{int} & t.type = \text{int} \\ \hline t \rightarrow \text{float} & t.type = \text{float} \\ \hline v \rightarrow v_1\ \text{id} & \begin{array}{l} v_1.type = v.type \\ \text{put(id.entry, v.type)} \end{array} \\ \hline v \rightarrow \text{id} & \text{put(id.entry, v.type)} \\ \hline \end{array} \] \[
